description: Pre-seeded agent documents for BEACON-CV trial rescue quorum. Each document contains realistic problems that agents must surface and resolve collaboratively.

problems: DEPENDENCY CONFLICT: Site Activation (t3) ends 2026-06-15 but IRB Approval (t2) ends 2026-06-30 — sites cannot activate before IRB approves. t3 must depend on t2, not just t1.
IMPOSSIBLE MILESTONE: First Patient Enrolled (t4) requires both t2 AND t3, but t3 completes before t2. The earliest possible date is 2026-06-30, not 2026-06-01.

INSUFFICIENT SAFETY DATA: DSMB Review (t6) starts only 6 weeks after first patient enrolled. FDA guidance recommends minimum 3 months of safety data before interim review for cardiovascular endpoints.
CASCADE DELAY: Correcting t3 dependency will push t4 to late June, t5 to Q4 2026, and t7 past the Q2 2027 hard stop.

line_items.notes: Scope change not budgeted — CRO added 2 FTEs for enhanced monitoring
Additional assays requested by Safety Monitor not in original scope
Lower than expected dosing due to enrollment shortfall
Assumes 12 visits/site — valid only if all 6 sites remain active
Amendment PA-001 and PA-002 fees not in original budget
NOT YET ALLOCATED despite known overruns — architect must approve release

problems: BUDGET OVERRUN: Total variance is -$90,000 before contingency release. Net position is only safe if contingency is partially deployed.
UNALLOCATED CONTINGENCY: $151,000 contingency exists but has not been allocated despite $90K in known overruns. This is a governance gap — who approves contingency release?
CRO SCOPE CREEP: CRO Management Fee overrun ($70K) accounts for 78% of total overrun. A contract amendment or scope negotiation is required before further spend.

CROSS-DOCUMENT CONFLICT: Additional bioanalytical costs ($30K, item 3) were requested by Safety Monitor but the Protocol Amendment Tracker (PA-002) also projects $30K lab cost impact — these may be double-counted.
TIMELINE DEPENDENCY: If enrollment extends per the timeline conflict, monitoring visit costs will increase proportionally — current $200K figure is understated.
AMENDMENT FEES: Regulatory/IRB overrun ($15K) confirms protocol amendments are in progress, but their scope and cost impact is not fully reflected here.

amendments.title: Dosing Schedule Modification
amendments.description: Change from BID to QD dosing based on PK data from first 30 patients. Reduces visit burden but requires updated consent language and revised DSMB monitoring plan.

amendments.title: Additional Biomarker Collection
amendments.description: Add exploratory biomarker collection (IL-6, TNF-alpha, hsCRP) at visits 3, 6, and 9. Adds approximately 30 minutes per visit.

amendments.title: eGFR Inclusion Criterion Relaxation
amendments.description: Proposed relaxation of eGFR inclusion threshold from >60 to >45 mL/min/1.73m². Based on site coordinator data showing 30% of otherwise-eligible patients excluded by current criterion.

problems: CONSENT NOT REVISED: PA-001 is pending IRB approval but its informed consent language has not been revised (consent_revised=false). Patients may currently be enrolling under outdated consent — potential regulatory violation.
SITES NOT NOTIFIED: Neither PA-001 nor PA-002 shows sites_notified=true. Sites are activating based on the original protocol, not the amended version.

TIMELINE CONFLICT: PA-001's target_approval (2026-04-15) matches the Site Activation start date in the Gantt. If IRB review takes longer (historically 6-8 weeks from submission), Site Activation will be blocked.

BUDGET DOUBLE-COUNT RISK: PA-002 projects $30K lab impact, but the Budget Analysis line item 3 (Lab/Bioanalytical, -$30K variance) appears to capture the same cost. Agents should verify whether these are the same or additive.

CUMULATIVE PATIENT BURDEN: PA-002 adds 30 min/visit at 3 visits = 1.5 additional hours per patient. Combined with the existing visit burden complaints from site coordinators, this is a material retention risk.

PA-003 DEPENDENCY: eGFR relaxation (PA-003) requires a DSMB charter update per IRB Officer's earlier statement, but this dependency is not captured in the amendment record. Missing: phased rollout plan, interim safety review at n=50.

AMENDMENT SEQUENCING: Submitting PA-001 (dosing), PA-002 (biomarkers), and PA-003 (eGFR) in parallel may overwhelm IRB review capacity and introduce conflicting consent addenda. Sequencing strategy needed.
